header.p: pdfml · M1 Typography Test Suite · of

footer.p: Generated by pdfml — pure Go PDF generation · Page

p.small: A comprehensive verification document for the M1 typography polish milestone.

body.p: Expected: Each line below (except the last in each paragraph) should be stretched to fill the full column width by expanding inter-word gaps. The final line of every paragraph must remain left-aligned.

article.p: Typography is the art and technique of arranging type to make written language legible, readable, and appealing when displayed. The arrangement of type involves selecting typefaces, point sizes, line lengths, line spacing, and letter spacing, and adjusting the space between pairs of letters. The term typography is also applied to the style, arrangement, and appearance of the letters, numbers, and symbols created by the process.

Justified text aligns both the left and right edges of each line by adjusting the spacing between words. It is commonly used in newspapers, magazines, and books because it creates a clean, rectangular block of text. However, it can produce uneven spacing — sometimes called rivers of white — especially in narrow columns. This short final line should not be justified.
A single-line paragraph. This line must not be justified.

body.p: Expected: The heading above and the styled spans below should show visibly wider gaps between individual characters.

Normal text for comparison: NORMAL WEIGHT and regular weight side by side.
1pt spacing: Hello World — inter-character spacing applied here.
Label style (1.5pt + uppercase): Section Header Label
Negative check — zero spacing should look identical to the default rendering above.
Expected: The nowrap span below must overflow its container as a single unbroken line rather than wrapping, even if the content is wide.
Normal text before the nowrap span. This entire sentence has white-space:nowrap and must not break across lines no matter how wide it gets. Normal text resumes after the nowrap span.

Expected: The parent block has line-height: 2.0. All children — regardless of their own font-size — should inherit the ratio (2×), not the resolved pt value from the parent. Larger text should have proportionally larger line spacing; smaller text should have proportionally smaller line spacing.

div.p: Base text (10pt, line-height = 2.0 × 10pt = 20pt). The spacing below this line should be visibly double the font size.
Back to 10pt. Notice the heading above had correct spacing relative to its own size, not a fixed 20pt inherited from the parent.
Mixed inline: normal text, small text at 0.85× (8.5pt × 2.0 = 17pt leading), superscript and subscript at 0.75× — all should sit on the same baseline with the correct line height.

body.p: Expected: All inline elements below should render correctly in a single paragraph without breaking the line flow.
Bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, highlighted, small, superscript, subscript, A Citation, a quoted phrase, inline code, and s p a c e d — all in one paragraph.
Mixed sizes on one line test for baseline alignment: normal (11pt) — small (9.35pt) — BOLD (11pt) — sup (8.25pt) — back to normal.
Expected: Each line should show the transformation applied to the same source text.

Source: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
uppercase: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
capitalize: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
lowercase: THE QUICK BROWN FOX JUMPS OVER THE LAZY DOG

p.u: This text has underline decoration — a line should appear below every word.

p.s: This text has line-through decoration — a line should cross the middle of every word.

body.p: Combined inline: buy old price €49.99 now only €29.99 — limited time offer!
Expected: Indentation, alignment, and blank lines inside the pre block must be preserved exactly as written.
body.pre: Column 1 Column 2 Column 3 --------- --------- --------- alpha beta gamma one two three

dl.dd: The process of adjusting the spacing between individual character pairs in proportional fonts to achieve a visually pleasing result.
The vertical distance between baselines of successive lines of text, derived from the strips of lead once placed between lines of metal type.
The uniform adjustment of spacing throughout a block of text, as opposed to kerning which targets specific character pairs. Also called letter-spacing in CSS.
The invisible line upon which most letters sit. Descenders of letters such as g, p, and y extend below the baseline.

table.caption: Table 1 — CSS Typography Properties Supported in M1

figure.p: Aa Bb Cc Dd Ee Ff Gg
figure.figcaption: Figure 1 — Liberation Sans glyph samples at multiple sizes and letter-spacing values.

blockquote.p: The details are not the details. They make the design. — Charles Eames

body.p: Inline usage: As Robert Bringhurst wrote in The Elements of Typographic Style, the typographer's one essential task is to interpret and communicate the text. The q element wraps content in curly quotation marks automatically.

ul.li: Justify — expands inter-word spacing to fill the line width.
Float layout — explicitly out of scope for v0.x.
Ratio-based line-height: children re-resolve against their own font-size.

ol.li: Parse XML into a DOM tree.
Resolve CSS cascade and compute styles.
Build the box tree and run layout.

body.p: In metal typesetting, compositors adjusted spacing using thin wedges called quoins. Modern digital typesetters use algorithms — from the simple greedy approach used here to the sophisticated Knuth-Plass dynamic programming method — to break paragraphs into lines and distribute leftover space. The goal in all cases is the same: produce lines that are visually even, avoiding unsightly gaps while keeping word spacing within acceptable bounds. The final line of this paragraph is intentionally short and must not be justified.

Short words: a is to of in on at by if or so do go me he we us be am it up no an as ox ax am ox. The spacing in this line should still be even despite the many single-character words that are hard to break gracefully.

p.small: End of M1 Typography Test · pdfml · pages total
